name: Adapter/Facade for Legacy Systems

useCases.use: SAP ECC material master lookups from cloud-native order management
useCases.dontUse: Greenfield services with no legacy system dependencies

useCases.use: AS/400 production scheduling queries from factory dashboard applications
useCases.dontUse: Direct database replication scenarios handled by CDC pipelines

useCases.use: Legacy MES data extraction for real-time quality analytics
useCases.dontUse: Legacy system decommissioning where the adapter would be short-lived (< 6 months)

components: Azure App Service — adapter runtime hosting with auto-scaling
Azure API Management — facade API exposure with contract versioning
Azure Cache for Redis — response caching to reduce legacy system load
Polly (.NET) / Resilience4j (Java) — circuit-breaker and retry library
Azure Service Bus — request buffering for throttled legacy backends
Application Insights — adapter health monitoring and legacy call telemetry

guardrails.rule: Circuit-breaker must be implemented for all legacy system calls
Response caching must be enabled with TTL appropriate to data freshness needs
Legacy credentials must be stored in Key Vault with managed identity access
Adapter API contract must be versioned independently of the legacy system
Circuit-breaker state changes must generate alerts

revisionHistory.author: Karen Mitchell
revisionHistory.summary: Initial draft with SAP ECC and AS/400 adapter patterns, circuit-breaker and caching guidance

strategicIntent: Provide an abstraction layer that wraps legacy on-premises systems (SAP ECC, AS/400, legacy MES) behind modern REST/gRPC interfaces with built-in throttling, circuit-breakers, and data mapping to enable gradual modernization.
exceptionProcess: Submit Integration Architecture Board request for direct legacy system access bypassing the adapter layer. Must include capacity impact analysis on legacy system and rollback plan.
interactionSteps: Consumer calls modern REST/gRPC endpoint exposed through API Management
Adapter checks Redis cache for recent responses to reduce legacy calls
On cache miss, adapter translates request to legacy protocol (BAPI, RFC, ODBC)
Circuit-breaker monitors legacy system response times and error rates
If legacy system is degraded, circuit-breaker opens and returns cached/fallback response
Excess requests are buffered in Service Bus queue for rate-limited processing
Application Insights tracks adapter latency, cache hit ratio, and circuit-breaker state
problemStatement: Direct integration with legacy systems exposes consumers to proprietary protocols (BAPI, RFC, ODBC), fragile interfaces, and capacity constraints that cause cascading failures when modern cloud workloads exceed legacy system throughput limits.
securityConsiderations: Legacy system credentials must be stored in Azure Key Vault, never in adapter config
Adapter must sanitize all inputs before passing to legacy systems to prevent injection
Cached data must respect the sensitivity classification of the source system
Network connectivity to on-premises legacy systems must use ExpressRoute or VPN, not public internet
Adapter logs must not contain legacy system credentials or sensitive query parameters
